Article Title: What Is Magic City Night? How Atlanta Hawks are Linked to the infamous strip club
Article Text:
Luke Kornet slams Atlanta Hawks' Magic City Night. Image via: NBA.com

What Is Magic City Night ? Here's what to know about the Atlanta Hawks' link with night club



Why Luke Kornet blasted the infamous strip club partnership



The Atlanta Hawks have ignited debate across the NBA after announcing a themed home game tied to one of the city’s most recognizable nightlife brands. The event, scheduled for March 16 against the Orlando Magic, is titled “Magic City Night” and is designed to celebrate a venue long woven into Atlanta’s music and sports culture.The promotion quickly drew national attention. While the franchise describes the night as a tribute to local culture, critics argue that the connection to an adult entertainment venue raises questions about league standards and messaging. One active player has already asked the NBA to intervene.Magic City opened in 1985 and has grown into a cultural landmark in Atlanta. The club is widely referenced in hip-hop lyrics and has hosted prominent athletes and entertainers for decades.Beyond its adult entertainment identity, it is known for its kitchen, especially its lemon pepper wings, which gained added fame during the 2020 NBA bubble era.For “Magic City Night,” the Hawks plan to spotlight that cultural footprint. The in-arena experience will include themed merchandise, food inspired by the club’s menu and appearances from Atlanta music figures, including rapper T.I.. Team executives framed the collaboration as a celebration of the city’s identity and creative influence.The official materials emphasize culture and community. They do not prominently describe the venue as a strip club, though that association remains central to the public conversation. Social media reactions have ranged from praise for embracing local flavor to criticism over appropriateness in a family sports setting.Luke Kornet of the San Antonio Spurs publicly called for the event to be canceled. In an open letter, he wrote, “The NBA should desire to protect and esteem women, many of whom work diligently every day to make this the best basketball league in the world.” He also said the league should promote “an atmosphere that is protective and respectful of the daughters, wives, sisters, mothers, and partners that we know and love. ”Kornet continued that allowing the night to proceed “without protest would reflect poorly on us as an NBA community,” and further warned about “the potential objectification and mistreatment of women in our society.” He added that many women in adult entertainment “experience abuse, harassment, and violence to which they should never be subjected.”He later wrote that teams “desire to provide an environment where fans of all ages can safely come and enjoy the game of basketball,” and concluded that celebrating a strip club “is not conduct aligned with that vision.”As of now, the Hawks have not announced changes to their plans. The league has also not issued a formal response, leaving the spotlight firmly on March 16.
Article Summary: The Atlanta Hawks have announced a themed home game tied to a nightlife brand. The event, scheduled for March 16 against the Orlando Magic, is titled “Magic City Night” Critics argue that the connection to an adult entertainment venue raises questions about league standards.

Article Title: Who is Elijah Hoard? Spurs star Victor Wembanyama sends worrying message for 23-year-old friend
Article Text:
Victor Wembanyama. Image via: Michael Gonzales/ Getty Images

Who is Victor Wembanyama's friend, Elijah Hoard?



Spurs star Victor Wembanyama asks for help to locate Elijah Hoard



San Antonio Spurs star Victor Wembanyama has issued a public plea after his 23-year-old friend, Elijah Hoard, was reported missing in Chicago. Hoard was last seen on February 27 at O’Hare International Airport and did not board his scheduled flight back to France. Chicago police have launched a missing person investigation and warned that he may need medical attention.Wembanyama shared the alert with his large social media following, asking the public to help locate “our friend.” The situation has drawn attention across both the NBA community and international basketball circles, as family members and authorities continue their search.Elijah Joseph Hoard, 23, lives in France and recently spent 10 days visiting his father, Antwon Hoard, in Chicago.According to local reports, his father dropped him off at O’Hare for a return flight to France. Hoard never checked in and did not board the plane.Police said he was last seen wearing a brown hooded sweatshirt, black sweatpants and cream-colored shoes. Authorities also noted he “may be in need of medical attention.” His phone has reportedly been off since Friday night, and family members believe he does not have enough money to remain on his own for long.Antwon Hoard described a shift in his son’s behavior before the scheduled departure. “Everything seemed pretty normal the first eight days, but the last two days his whole mood had swung, and he was really adamant about not going back home, and he didn't want to go home,” Antwon explained. He added that Elijah may still be somewhere around the airport area. “But again, that's the last we've seen of him.”Elijah is also known in basketball circles. He previously played at the LDLC ASVEL academy in France and continued competing in lower tiers, and he is the brother of Maccabi Tel Aviv's forward Jaylen Hoard. He is listed at 6-foot-3 and around 220 pounds.Victor Wembanyama turned to Instagram to widen the search. In his post, he wrote: “Our friend Elijah has been missing from Chicago O'Hare since February 27th.If you have any information, please reach out to local authorities.”The Spurs center also tagged Chicago sports figures, including Derrick Rose, Joakim Noah, and Connor Bedard, hoping to amplify the message. His outreach quickly spread across social media.Chicago police are reviewing surveillance footage from O’Hare as part of the investigation. Family members, including Elijah’s sister Leah, have urged the public to share information in both English and French to reach a wider audience.Anyone with information is asked to contact the Chicago Police Missing Persons Unit.
Article Summary: Elijah Joseph Hoard, 23, lives in France and recently spent 10 days visiting his father in Chicago. Hoard was last seen on February 27 at O’Hare International Airport and did not board his scheduled flight back to France. Chicago police have launched a missing person investigation and warned that he may need medical attention.

Article Title: Washington Wizards get interesting Trae Young news ahead of Anthony Davis’ highly anticipated debut
Article Text:
Trae Young. Image via: Dale Zanine-Imagn Images

Trae Young is set to make Washington Wizards debut



When will Trae Young and Anthony Davis share the court with the Wizards?



Anthony Davis thinks he and Trae Young can become a dynamic duo (Images via Getty)

The Washington Wizards are preparing for a key moment in their rebuild. Trae Young is expected to make his team debut this week, months after arriving in a headline trade. His return comes after knee and quadriceps injuries limited him to 10 games earlier this season with the Atlanta Hawks. Washington acquired the four-time All-Star in January, sending CJ McCollum and Corey Kispert to Atlanta in a move that reshaped the franchise’s direction. Anthony Davis , the other centerpiece addition before the deadline, remains sidelined with a hand injury. His timeline is still uncertain. Yet Young’s imminent return signals that the organization plans to see its new stars on the court before the regular season closes.The long wait is nearing its end. ESPN insider Shams Charania reported, “Washington Wizards star Trae Young will make his team debut on Thursday against the Utah Jazz at home.”Young also posted a workout clip in Wizards gear and ended it with “3/5,” pointing to the expected return date.Head coach Brian Keefe confirmed that Young is trending toward playing against Utah. He indicated the guard would likely be limited to around 17 to 20 minutes, mostly in the first half. The team plans to manage his workload carefully after months of recovery.Young averaged 19.3 points and 8.9 assists in 10 games earlier this season before injuries halted his progress.Washington’s front office understood the risk when it made the trade. Many around the league believed Young might sit out the remainder of the year. Instead, he appears ready to take the floor in front of his new home crowd.The debut offers more than box score production. It allows the franchise to begin building chemistry and evaluate how its young core fits alongside an elite playmaker.The larger question centers on Anthony Davis. Washington previously stated he would be re-evaluated two weeks after the initial update on his hand injury, and that checkpoint is approaching. Davis has not yet been cleared for basketball activities, though he has joined the team in Washington and attended practice sessions.General manager Will Dawkins has maintained that the goal is to have both Young and Davis appear before the season ends. Young’s clearance suggests the team remains committed to that plan. It also signals that Washington values early on-court reps over draft positioning.If Davis receives a positive medical update, he could join Young for limited minutes down the stretch. The Wizards would then get their first look at the pairing they envisioned at the deadline. However, as of now, there is no timeline for the duo sharing the Wizard court.
Article Summary: Trae Young is set to make his Washington Wizards debut against the Utah Jazz on Thursday. Young has been sidelined with knee and quadriceps injuries. Anthony Davis remains sidelined with a hand injury. The Wizards hope to have both players on the court before the season ends.

Article Title: NBA contract update: Free agent guard makes new move with Denver Nuggets after traded twice at the deadline
Article Text:
Tyus Jones. Image via: Jerome Miron-Imagn Images

Tyus Jones reportedly signs with the Denver Nuggets



Tyus Jones was waived by the Dallas Mavericks



The Denver Nuggets have moved quickly to address a clear roster need. Free agent guard Tyus Jones has signed with Denver after a turbulent stretch that saw him traded twice at the deadline and later waived. ESPN’s Shams Charania reported the deal, which gives the 29-year-old a fresh opportunity with a Western Conference contender.Jones’ path to Denver has been anything but stable. He opened the season with the Orlando Magic before being dealt to the Charlotte Hornets and then rerouted to the Dallas Mavericks. Dallas waived him to create room for two-way guard Ryan Nembhard. Now, with Denver seeking steadiness behind All-Star Jamal Murray, Jones steps into a situation where his playmaking history could fill a pressing gap.Denver’s interest reflects a specific need. The Nuggets average 6.5 assists per game from their bench, the third-fewest in the league. That lack of second-unit creation has lingered all season. Across 11 NBA seasons, Jones has built a reputation as a careful floor general with a strong assist-to-turnover ratio.In eight games with Dallas, he averaged 8.1 assists per 36 minutes and posted a 6.0 assist-to-turnover ratio, the third-best mark in the NBA during that stretch.Those numbers show he can still organize an offense in short bursts. Over his career, Jones has averaged 7.3 points and 4.3 assists. His most productive run came with the Memphis Grizzlies in the 2021-22 and 2022-23 seasons, when he averaged 9.5 points and 4.8 assists across 153 games and entered discussions for the Sixth Man of the Year award.Denver views him as a reliable ball-handler who can maintain tempo when Murray sits. The Nuggets have added depth before, yet the need for a steady reserve point guard remained. Jones now has a defined role: manage possessions, limit turnovers, and support a playoff rotation.Jones’ signing comes after a difficult year. Expectations were high when he joined Orlando on a one-year deal. Instead, he averaged 3.0 points and 2.4 assists in 48 appearances, shooting 34.2 percent from the field and 29.4 percent from three. His production fell from the previous season with the Phoenix Suns, where he averaged 10.2 points and 5.3 assists while shooting 44.8 percent overall and 41.4 percent from beyond the arc.He was traded to Charlotte at the deadline and later moved to Dallas. His stint with the Mavericks did not reverse the trend. He averaged 3.9 points and 3.8 assists in 16.6 minutes per game, shooting 38.2 percent from the field and 21.1 percent from three before being waived.With few options emerging, Denver offered Jones a new path. The Nuggets now hope his experience and ball security translate into more stable bench production as the season moves forward. As of now, the exact value of Jones’ new contract is not known.
Article Summary: Tyus Jones reportedly signs with the Denver Nuggets. Jones was waived by the Dallas Mavericks. The 29-year-old was traded twice at the deadline and later waived. Denver views him as a reliable ball-handler who can maintain tempo when Jamal Murray sits.

Article Title: “I’ll just insert myself”: Stephen Curry’s wife Ayesha Curry on confronting women flirting with him in front of her
Article Text:
Stephen Curry’s wife Ayesha Curry on confronting women flirting with him in front of her.(Image via Getty Images)

Stephen Curry’s wife, Ayesha Curry, reflects on how she stops women from flirting with him



Stephen Curry has faced no cheating rumors in his over a decade long marriage to Ayesha Curry



Stephen Curry, the Golden State Warriors’ star player, has always been supportive of his wife, Ayesha Curry , through all the controversial comments she has made in the last few years.While Stephen Curry continues to focus on his career in the league, his wife, Ayesha Curry, has often shared a glimpse of what happens at home. Amid this, Ayesha Curry shared how she stops women from pursuing her husband.A few years ago, in 2019, in a conversation with Jada Pinkett Smith, Ayesha Curry addressed the female attention that her husband, the NBA legend Stephen Curry, receives on a regular basis.While she made it clear that she “hates” it, Ayesha Curry also shared how she stops such women.The popular entrepreneur said, "I'm a grown woman so I'll just insert myself. I'll be like, ‘Hello. How are you doing?'"Stephen Curry’s wife, Ayesha Curry, also opened up about how most of the times the Golden State Warriors’ star player, is unaware if a woman has been flirting with him.To make things worse, this was not the first time that Ayesha Curry addressed the female attention her husband receives.In another conversation with PEOPLE, Stephen Curry’s wife, Ayesha Curry, reflected when she first crossed paths with him in church and he was always “surrounded by girls”.Despite the female attention that Stephen Curry has received over the years, there have never been any cheating rumors about his marriage to Ayesha Curry.Stephen Curry and Ayesha Curry got married in 2011 after dating for a few years and are now parents to four children.The Golden State Warriors’ star player has continued to be deeply committed and in love with his wife and their four children as he seems pretty content with his personal life.However, this has not stopped fans from raising questions about their marriage as many have trolled Ayesha Curry over some of her controversial remarks.Many have brutally trolled Ayesha Curry as questions on her commitment to the NBA legend have been raised.But the two remained silent as they kept on focusing to keep their children away from the drama and the spotlight as they continue to get trolled for the love and commitment they have for one another.
Article Summary: Stephen Curry has faced no cheating rumors in his over a decade long marriage to Ayesha Curry. The Golden State Warriors’ star player has continued to be deeply committed and in love with his wife and their four children. However, this has not stopped fans from raising questions about their marriage as many have trolled AYESha Curry over some of her controversial remarks.

Article Title: Luke Kornet: San Antonio Spurs star Luke Kornet’s bold stand against Hawks’ Magic City Night sparks heated and emotional NBA debate
Article Text:
Luke Kornet is facing controversy now (Image via NBA)

Luke Kornet speaks out against Hawks’ Magic City Night as debate grows across the NBA

Spurs big man Luke Kornet urges Hawks to cancel Magic City Night as reactions split fans

Luke Kornet's shocking decision to pull off the Hawks' planned Magic City Night celebration just caused mayhem in the NBA. The San Antonio Spurs center denounced the March 16 event as demeaning to women in a strong Medium blog piece earlier today. The league as a whole is now commenting on the dispute.Atlanta's contentious theme night premiered on March 16 when the Hawks faced the Orlando Magic. As the squad honors the city's storied strip club, "Magic City Monday" is anticipated to be a major attraction for Atlantans. Kornet's worries are not unique. He claims that the Hawks' choice shocked other players in the league as well.One basketball player is adamantly against the Eastern Conference team's plans, despite the Atlanta Hawks' recent announcement of their "Magic City Monday" theme night at one of their upcoming NBA games becoming viral.That institution is Magic City, the strip club that is well-known for, among other reasons, lemon pepper chicken wings so delicious that Lou Williams had to skip NBA bubble games because he stopped in before heading to Orlando.Kornet stressed that the NBA should value and safeguard the women who regularly support the sport. He voiced serious concern about the mistreatment and harassment of employees in the adult entertainment sector. In a statement supporting women's rights, Kornet implied that the NBA wouldn't benefit from the game's topic. Kornet said Monday:“The NBA should desire to protect and esteem women, many of whom work diligently every day to make this the best basketball league in the world... We should promote an atmosphere that is protective and respectful of the daughters, wives, sisters, mothers, and partners that we know and love…”He continued:“Regardless of how a woman finds her way into the adult entertainment industry, many in this space experience abuse, harassment, and violence to which they should never be subjected."Kornet said. He also pointed out that the event could reflect poorly on the NBA as a whole, making the league look “complicit in the potential objectification and mistreatment of women in our society.”In social media and sporting circles, the statement has already generated intense controversy. Luke Kornet of the San Antonio Spurs, however, has voiced his disapproval of the celebration and urged the league to "protect and esteem women." Jami Gertz is adamant about the promotion.Magic City, according to the Hawks owner, is an "iconic cultural institution" that influenced the development of hip-hop and Atlanta music. She produced "Magic City: An American Fantasy," a five-part STARZ documentary that examined the venue's history.There are strippers in Magic City in addition to the kitchen, which is why some people object to the promotion. Luke Kornet, a big guy for the Spurs, is one of those individuals. On Monday, he penned an open letter to the league requesting that the Hawks call off the event.The celebration gains a local character from the Lou Williams-named lemon-pepper wings. Rapper T.I., who will perform during the Hawks game, is one of the prominent Atlanta people scheduled to appear on "Magic City Monday." For many years, the Magic City nightclub has been well-known for its popular wings and women.Although the Hawks have not yet addressed Kornet's remarks, the incident has undoubtedly generated discussion. While some fans, like Kornet, think it's a move in the wrong direction, others see it as an enjoyable way to showcase Atlanta's culture.
Article Summary: The San Antonio Spurs center denounced the March 16 event as demeaning to women in a strong Medium blog piece. Kornet stressed that the NBA should value and safeguard the women who regularly support the sport. He also pointed out that the event could reflect poorly on the NBA as a whole, making the league look “complicit in the potential objectification and mistreatment of women in our society"
